title: pilosella bauhini subsp. hypermelana
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. hypermelana, also known as Pilosella aurantiaca, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant within the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. hypermelana include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and combat infections, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Pilosella bauhini subsp. hypermelana can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used to create topical remedies for wound healing and skin conditions.
